fields.helpText: There is only one 'number' in the MAS Parameters File at this time, and that is the number 'one' denoting there is only on MAS Parameter. The labels are subfields of this one parameter.
'One' designates the 'one' MAS Parameter.  There can only be one set of parameters.
Does your facility have a Hemodialysis Ward?
Enter number of seconds to wait for user to respond to prompts (at least 10 seconds).  Enter 99999 if you wish no system timeout.

Enter the number of days you want to save G&L corrections in the system (between 14 and 365); corrections older than this number are deleted by the system when G&L Auto-Recalculation runs.  Leave blank if you wish to maintain the complete file.  Up to 90 days is recommended.
Enter the number of hours you want an active registration to remain open before the disposition is considered late (between 0 and 240).
Do you wish Supplemental 10-10 to be printed wtih 10-10?
Do you wish to have printing of drug profiles prompted with 10-10 printing?

If you answered YES to the 'PRINT PTF MESSAGES?' prompt select the default device to which these message should print.  This device will be used in cases where a specific device is not entered for the individual facility/division using the 'DEVICE SELECTION' option of the SYSTEM DEFINITION MENU.

Enter '1' if you wish to print PTF messages upon admission, change of treating specialty or deletion of admission, treating specialty transfer, discharge or 501 movement.  Enter '0' if you DO NOT wish PTF messages to print.

This field indicates whether the site wants to automatically generate PTF messages as part of normal ADT processing.   If 'YES' than entries into the PTF MESSAGE file will occur. If 'NO' than no PTF messages will be generated during ADT activities.
Enter the earliest date for G&L to be run on.  The day previous to this will be used for ward bed status data initialization.
Is your facility multi-divisional?
Enter name of primary facility for which you are defining parameters.
Is your facility affiliated with a teaching facility?
This field is currently unused by the MAS package and will be deleted in a future release of MAS.  It was previously used to determine whether the honeywell software should be utilized or not.
Does your site have nursing home wards?
Does your medical center have domiciliary wards?
Determines if HINQ inquiry can be made on registration.
Do you wish to have embossing of data card prompted during registration options?
Do you wish closest printer to be default for 10-10s, routing slips and pharmacy profiles?
If a 'Default Code Sheet Printer' is defined, AMIS code sheets can be generated when AMIS(s) are generated. The Code Sheets created will be printed on the default printer.
During the editing of a disposition, should the user default answer be 'Yes' or 'No' to the following prompt:      'Do you wish to see the check out screen?'
Enter number of days for which sensitivity data must be maintained on line before purging is permitted.
Do you want the MAS Status Screen to be displayed to users upon entering the ADT Menu?

Do you wish to turn on the automatic Consistency Checker?  If YES, data will be checked in accordance with parameters set through the Determine Consistencies to Check/Don't Check option of the Consistency Supervisor Menu.

Do you wish an abbreviated patient inquiry to be displayed through the Patient Inquiry Option?  An abbreviated inquiry will include the following:  name, SSN, date of birth, address, temporary address, means test status, eligibility status, admission and appointment enrollment information.  Items such as religion, claim number and period of service will be excluded.

Answering YES will force the DEVICE: prompt at the beginning of registration the first time through and will set the 1010, routing sheet, and profile printer to this device.  This takes precedence over all devices defined as default printers or closet printer.
This will prevent the Co-Pay menus and routines from being invoked by the  Registration software.

By answering 'YES' the user will be prompted to print a Health Summary at the end of the Registration process and during the  10/10 Print without New Registration option.  NOTE: Unless HEALTH SUMMARY v2.5 patch #3 (or higher) has been loaded the user will not be prompted to print a Health Summary.

When the user is prompted to print a Health Summary type in Registration, this field contains the default.  NOTE: Unless the MAS PARAMETER, 'PRINT HEALTH SUMMARY?', has been answered 'YES', this has no effect.

If YES is entered for the PRINT DRUG PROFILES WITH 10-10 field, answering YES to this field will offer the user a choice between the Action and Informational Drug Profiles.  Note: Unless the PRINT DRUG PROFILES WITH 10-10 has been answered 'YES', this field will have no effect.

This field is irrelevant unless the PRINT DRUG PROFILES WITH 10-10 field has been answered 'YES'. If the user has a choice of Drug Profiles (when CHOICE OF DRUG PROFILE TYPE? is YES) this field defines the default.  If the user does not have a choice of Drug Profiles, this field defines the type of Drug Profile (Action or Informational).
After this number of days without updating the DATE MEDICAID LAST ASKED field an inconsistency will be created.  The default is 365 days.
Answering "YES" will have the prompt Print Encounter Form be asked at Registration.  If the encounter form is said to be  printed,  the clinic(s) to print the encounter forms will be asked.
This field contains the name of the default printer that will print encounter forms at registration.
The date/time the Gains and Losses Sheet was started by a user through the ADT Outputs menu option.
The date/time G&L recalculation was started.  A user would recalc a G&L for past dates normally.
Date G&L recalculation has reached cleared when recalc is finished.
The task number of the G&L recalculation job running.
The date/time the G&L recalculation is scheduled to run.  It is a nightly background job usually set to run sometime after midnight each day.
CPU recalc was started from
The date/time that Auto Recalc was last started for the G&L stats.
CPU which last ran auto recalc
When the G&L Auto Recalc last ran, this is the date it went back to for recalculation of the stats.
Time G&L was run last.

A site may need to capture certain registration data in a format other than that of the distributed registration screens.  It may do so by creating a local registration screen (through a FileMan template) specifying that data in the PATIENT file they wish to capture.  The template name should be specified at this prompt.  This screen will then be prompted during the registration or load/edit process at the end of the other registration screens.

Should high intensity be used for those options in MAS which allow it? This feature works in conjunction with the Registration and MCCR screens to signify to users that data which may/may not be edited. Under certain circumstances some screens or individual data groups may only be edited by selected users.  When this feature is turned on, those screens and/or data group numbers which a specific user may edit will be 'highlighted' on their terminal but not 'highlighted' to those users who may not

If the USE HIGH INTENSITY ON SCREENS field has been set to YES, you may wish to exclude it from being used on certain terminal types. If so, at this prompt you should specify the terminal type(s) which should not use high intensity.  This field allows you to specify multiple terminal types.  You will be returned to it repeatedly until no more entries are made.
The date the purging of the INCONSISTENT DATA file was started.
The date the INCONSISTENT DATA purge last completed.
The date the INCONSISTENT DATA file was started rebuilding.
The date the INCONSISTENT DATA file last completed rebuilding.
The date updating of existing data in the INCONSISTENT DATA file was last started.
The date updating of existing data in the INCONSISTENT DATA file last completed running.
Date the overdue absence list was run.  An overdue absence list produces those veterans who have been placed on either AA<96 hrs. or AA and have not returned on the date entered as their return date.
This Multiple will contain parameters used for the conversion of MAS files from file 3, 6, and 16 to file 200.
This field contains a list of all entries that failed to be        converted during the Provider Conversion. The contents of this field will be used to generate a Mailman message at the end of the

This field contains the date of the current network workday.  This will usually be the current day's date. On days that the network is unavailable (weekends and holidays) this field will contain the value of the previous network workday's date. For example, on a Sunday, this field will contain the date of the previous Friday.   The value in this field is updated automatically by the system on  workday mornings.  It should NOT be necessary to edit this field.

This field contains the date of the network workday that is previous to the current network workday.  For example, on a Tuesday, it contains Monday's date.  On a Sunday, the network workday is still Friday so this field will contain Thursday's date.   When the parameter, NETWORK DAY, is updated on workdays, this field is automatically updated to the value of the previous NETWORK DAY.   VIEW THE HINQ SUSPENSE FILE option and PROCESS THE HINQ SUSPENSE FILE option both display entries beginning wit

This entry is derived from the time zone field of the Kernel parameter  file and the Mailman time zone file.  If these files are not  present it may be set to a positive number which is the difference  between GMT and the sites current time zone, ie for Eastern daylight  time it should be set to 4, Central daylight to 5, etc.    This field is used to check the time of availablity of the  DVB computer and disable appropriate HINQ options if the DVB computer  is not available.

This parameter enables or disables the options which transmit requests to the DVB computer - GENERATE A HINQ REQUEST and PROCESS THE HINQ SUSPENSE FILE.  Enter a YES to enable these options or a NO to disable them.   These options are automatically disabled during DVB computer non-workday hours.  However, it may be necessary to disable these options temporarily if transmission problems are occurring.

This parameter is set to a YES or NO by the system to enable or disable the option, PROCESS THE SUSPENSE FILE.  The option is automatically disabled during DVB computer non-workday hours. When the option is selected during workday hours and this field contains a NO, a test of the network is automatically performed.  This field will be set to a YES to enable the option when a successful test has been completed for the day.

This field will contain a YES or NO value that indicates whether or not the HINQ Suspense file contains 'P'ending entries. The system sets this field. If this field is set to NO, queued jobs will quit before logging into the remote DVB.  It may be manually reset to YES if there are pending entries in the suspense file.

This parameter should be set to indicate to the system whether or not requests with communications errors should automatically be retransmitted to the DVB computer.  Set this field to Y for YES REQUEUE or to N for NO REQUEUEING.   If this parameter is set to REQUEUE requests  will not be retransmitted if the Network is Disabled or the remote DVB computer is not available because of time, or sat,sunday,holidays.
This parameter should be set to a number between 0 and 50 to indicate to the system the total number of requeued jobs  that can be Retransmitted to the DVB computer in a day.

This field is a count of the number of batch processes that have been requeued by the system on a particular day. This field is incremented by the system each time a process  is automatically requeued to the DVB computer. It is reset to zero by the system on workdays.   To allow processes to be requeued, the number in this field must not be greater than the number in the AUTO-REQUEUE LIMIT field.  If necessary, this field can be reset to zero by the user to allow additional processes to be reque
This symbol will be inserted in the HINQ request depending on the geographical area of the station.  A corresponding symbol will be in the SIGNON table, and the CONFIGURATION facility of the MCTS.
NOT currently active. This parameter implements queing via the network mailman.  When it is set all Batch request are handled through the mailman's queues.

This field should contain the time difference between the site and the RDPC where HINQ requests are sent.  A site in the eastern time zone sending to the Midwest RDPC should enter '-1' here.  A site in the pacific time zone sending to the Midwest RDPC should enter '2' here.  If the RDPC is in the same time zone enter '0' here. Philadephia is East, Hines, Midwest, and Austin are in Central time zones.

When a user enters a request in the HINQ suspense file 39.5, if the site is multi-division as defined in ADT parameter file 43, field 11, the user will be prompted to enter a medical center division as defined in file 40.8.   When the HINQ request is processed, the top line of the response will show the division name to aid in patient identification.

This is the code for addressing the DVB computer through VADATS.  DMS.BDNE is for Philly  DMS.BDNC is for Hines  DMS.BDNW is for Austin  DMS.BDNM is for Midwest  To send HINQ requests to AUSTIN  SELECT VADATS CODE: DMS.BNDW and set the HINQ identifier, subfield 4,  and enter the DEVICE NAME, subfield 5.
This is the Version number of the currently loaded Scheduling DHCP software.
This field contains the dates, status, and summary information for the Scheduling archive process.

Enter the number of days in the past that the 'Appointment Management' option should initially search for appointments.   When the user selects a patient, this parameter will automatically be used to search that many days in the past for appointments.   When a clinic is selected, this number will be used to calculate the default beginning date.  The user is  prompted for a beginning and ending date when a clinic is selected.   If this field is not entered, then the system will use 2 days.

This field definition is used by the 'Appointment Management' option.  The option obtains a patient or clinic when using the FileMan Reader.  This field is specified in the Reader call.   No data needs to be set in this field.
This is the name of the Mail Group which should be notified whenever the Appointment Status Update job has been completed.
This is the name of the Mail Group which should be notified whenever the nightly NPCDB job has been completed.

This is the name of the Mail Group which should be notified whenever there is late entry/edit of NPCDB related information.   'Late entry' is defined as information entered after the workload close out date has passed.   For example, if an appointment is made after the close out date has passed for the appointment date then the members of this mail group will receive a message.

This field indicates the date the site must start checking out appointments, stop code additions (add/edits) and registrations (1010 and unscheduled). Once v5.3 is released then this date will be Oct. 1, 1993.   However, for alpha and beta testing, this date needs to be set before 10/1/93.

Enter 'YES' to allow users to up-arrow out of the classification questions.  The classification questions are required for workload credit. Allowing the user to exit without answering these questions could result in additional work at a later time to track down the record and determine the answer to these questions.

This field is used to indicate which Mail Group should receive any notifications produced by the Outpatient API.  Field 'API NOTIFICATIONS TO PROCESS (#227)' indicates the type of notifications should result in a MailMan message.

This field indicates which notification messages from the Outpatient API should be sent to the appropriate Mail Group. The Mail Group is specified in the 'API ERROR MAIL GROUP (#226)' field.   Possible answers are the following:                        ERRORS - send just error notifications             WARNINGS & ERRORS - send warning and error notifications                          NONE - do not send any notifications   Error Definition: ----------------- Before an event is processed, the data p

Enter in this field the year of the means test income category thresholds, property threshold and child income exclusion.  These figures are released annually.  They are effective on January 1st and are applied to the previous year income.
This data is now stored in Integrated Billing.  This field to be deleted with the next release of MAS after 5.2.   Costs by treating Service charged by the VA for care received at a VA facility.
Fiscal Year that inpatient and outpatient costs at a VA facility are in effect.
This is the current entry number in the ADT/HL7 Pivot file.  Only the  application should edit this field.
This field tells the software whether to stop sending the PIMS HL7 messages.  If set to 0 or stop no PIMS HL7 messages will be fired from the event drivers, DDs, or registration options.
This field tells the software whether to stop sending the PIMS HL7 v2.3 messages.  If set to 0 or stop no PIMS HL7 v2.3 messages will be fired from the event drivers, DDs, or registration options.

The number of days worth of data to retain in the ADT/HL7 PIVOT file (#391.71) when the  Purge PIMS HL7 PIVOT File option [VAFH PIVOT PURGE] is run.                                   Example: If this field is set to 100, then entries in file #391.71 which are older  than TODAY-100 days will be purged.                                   If nothing is entered in this field, then  a default value of 547 days will be used. (547 days is approximately 18 months.)
This field will indicate whether the site has integrated it's RAI/MDS COTS databases or not and to what level.  If the field is null, then the site has not integrated.
This field contains the date/time of the last run of the IRT Background Job.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever a patient expires.  If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever a new patient is added to the patient file.  If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever the spelling of an existing patients name is changed.  If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever the social security number of an existing patient is changed.  If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.

Select the name of the mailgroup to be notified along with the user, whenever a veteran is admitted for whom eligibility has not been verified, or for patients admitted with verified eligibility who have either future scheduled admissions or waiting list entries.   If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.

Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever inconsistencies are found in a patients database that can not be corrected by the user because of edit restrictions, i.e., not holding the 'DG ELIGIBILITY' security key for verified data.  If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever a non-veteran is admitted to the facility.  If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.

Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever the G&L is run and the user elects to search for patients who are overdue from returning from Authorized/Unauthorized absence and pass. If no mailgroup is select no bulletin will be generated.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever a patient is DELETED from the database (PATIENT file).  If no local mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.
Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever a sensitive record is accessed.  If no mailgroup is selected no notification will be made however the access will still be recorded.

Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever the status of a previously identified sensitive record is changed to non-sensitive.  If no mailgroup is selected no notification will be made.
Enter the name of the mail group that will receive the message stating a means test is required when an appointment is made.

Select the name of the mailgroup which should be notified whenever the IRT Background Job is run, options: (IRT Update Std. Deficiencies and IRT Update Std. Def. Background Job), to receive a list of patients that have been discharged less than 48 hours from their admission (Short Form).. If no mailgroup is selected no bulletin will be generated.
Date/time generation was started for the AMIS 420.
Date/time AMIS 420 generation was last completed.
System task number assigned to the job for the AMIS 420 generation.
Person/user that ran the generation for the AMIS 420.
Date/time the AMIS 420 is currently running.
Month and year the MAS Parameters file was checked/edited.
Date/time Resource Utilization Grouper (RUG) background job was last run.
Date the RUGs were converted to RUG17 methodology.

This will be used to print on the 70-3542d form, if the site decides that they want to have a set name printed all the time.  If it is left blank then the user's name, followed by DESIGNEE OF CERTIFYING OFFICIAL will be printed instead of the set name of the certifying official.
This field will be used to determine whether the "MEALS & LODGING" and "FERRY, BRIDGES, ETC." questions are asked in the Beneficiary Travel Claim Enter/Edit Option.
Do you want the patient's temporary address to be used on scheduling letters if one is provided in the PATIENT file?
This entry determines whether the Bene Travel package prompts for coreFLS vendor (ACTIVE - YES) or FMS vendor (ACTIVE - NO).
A list of all routines distributed with the MAS DHCP software package.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.
This field is no longer used in determining bill numbers.

Obsolete field no longer used. bill has been created, this field is automatically set back to null. However, if a system crash occurs during the bill creation process, this field should be set to null through fileman.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.

This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2. billing record is being amended. The generic remark, "BILL CANCELLED IN MAS" will be transmitted to Fiscal Service if no remark is entered in this field. The site may enter any remark which is meaningful to MAS and Fiscal. This remark will be transmitted each time a bill is cancelled in MAS.

This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release of MAS after 5.2.   Answering Yes to this parameter will also allow billing clerks to enter CPT and HCPCS codes into the billing record as well as ICD Diagnosis and Procedure codes that are not in the corresponding PTF record.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2. for bills on patients with unverified eligibility.
This field has been moved to the IB SITE PARAMETER file.  To be deleted with the next release after MAS 5.2.

Restartable entry point for means test conversion; last patient converted  This is a temporary field used during the means test conversion, only.  Field will be deleted in the following release, MAS 5.3.
Date means test conversion started.  This is a temporary field used during the means test conversion, only.  Field will be deleted in the following release, MAS 5.3.
Date means test conversion completed.  This is a temporary field used during  the means test conversion, only.  Field will be deleted in the following release, MAS 5.3.
The version number assigned to the MAS software package currently installed in the system.

Please enter the date on which you wish to initialize your  Gains & Losses Sheet and Bed Status Report.  The date selected  must be on or after October 1, 1990. Bed Status statistics will  be calculated from this date only.

The Gains & Losses Sheet allows printing only the last four  numbers of the patient's Social Security Number or the entire  number.  Please determine how you'd like this number to appear  on your G&L.
This G&L is designed to display current Means Test Status  (where applicable) if you wish.  Simply respond YES to this  prompt in order to display this data on your daily Gains  and Losses Sheet.

The This field is used for the purpose of determining whether  or not you wish for the exact Treating Specialty to which a  patient is assigned to display on the G&L whenever a movement  is displayed.  The information will appear immediately to the  right of the assigned ward location.   "turned-on" a two-column G&L will always be produced.

Please select whether you wish the patient listing (G&L)  to display names in a two or three column format.  Certain  transaction types, i.e., Transfers in/out, will always appear as a single column output regardless, however, this feature  may reduce the amount of paper required to generate this listing.
Answer YES should you wish for non-movements, i.e., Treating  Specialty Change to appear on the G&L otherwise answer NO.

Please enter the date from which you wish to recalculate totals which must be on or after the G&L Initialization Date.  The  purpose of this parameter is to allow you the flexibility  to maintain an "old" initialization date without the worry  that a correction entered say, for five years ago,  cause a recalculation process to commence which may take  days to complete.  It is strongly suggested that recalculation  always be accomplished for, at a minumum, the current fiscal  reporting year.

Answer YES if you wish to calculate vietnam era veteran's remaining  at the end of each day.  If you choose to do so the length  of processing time may be increased up to 20 minutes per day.   Please note that this information is no longer reported  on any AMIS Segment.

Answer YES to the following prompt if you wish to calculate  patient's over 65 years in age remaining at the end of each  census date.  If you choose to do so processing time for  recalculation may be increased up to 30 minutes per date. Please note that this information is no longer reported  on any AMIS Segment.

Once the system starts collecting Treating Specialty statistics it  will be necessary to capture those data where a Treating  Specialty may not have been assigned to a particular patient.     Please respond to to this field with the DEFAULT Treating Specialty to which you want these statistical data  associated.  Perhaps one might want to create a Treating  Specialty called UNKNOWN, UNDEFINED, etc., and utilize this.

Enter the date on which you wish to initialize your Treating Specialty  Report.  The date selected must be on or after October 1, 1992.   The Treating Specialty Report census statistics will be calculated  from this date.
This entry determines the sort order of the call list display

This field will be used to determine how many days from the latest DATE CHANGED Field, #1, in PRE-REGISTRATION AUDIT File, #41.41, before the patient will be added to the call list again.  If the patient has another appointment within x number of days of the date in the DATE CHANGED field, he will not be added.

fields.setOfCodes: D:DELETE ALL ENTRIES;P:DELETE CALLED PATIENTS;DA:DELETE ENTRIES AND ADD NEW;PA:DELETE CALLED PATIENTS AND ADD NEW;AO:ADD NEW ENTRIES ONLY;N:NOTHING;
fields.helpText: Preregistration nightly background job. This field determines which actions the background job performs when run.  Delete removes all entries from the call list.  Purge removes only the called patients from the call list.  Nothing tells the background job to leave the call list alone. Add, just adds entries to the call list, without removing any.
This field is the number of days to maintain entries in the PRE-REGISTRATION CALL LOG File, #41.43.  All entries before this limit will be purged.
This field sets the number of days in advance to look for appointments to pull patients for.
If this field is 'Y'es, then the background job will run and add on weekends.

When using the PreRegister Patient [DGPRE PreRegister Patient] option if "Increase Engagement in My HealtheVet" functionality is enabled then the clerk will be prompted to ask Veterans if they are enrolled in My HealtheVet and if not, to encourage them to enroll.   This is an optional field which only needs to be set to "YES" when a site desires to use this functionality. This field was added with Patch DG*5.3*903 (Increase Engagement in My HealtheVet during Pre-Registration).
If this parameter is set to 1 and the user does not hold the DG RECORD ACCESS security key, they will not be allowed to access their own PATIENT (#2) file record.

This field should be edited if the local site wants the daily tasked Purple Heart Status Report to be sorted in 'A'scending order by the number of days since the last status update.  By default, the report will sort by descending order.

This field is used as a flag to indicate the method to use in determining  the veteran's net worth.  If the Net Worth Calculation = "YES" then net  worth is equal to the veteran's assets minus debts.  If the Net Worth  Calculation = "NO" then net worth is equal to assets plus income minus  debts.

The date, January 1,2013, that the Veteran Financial Assessment(VFA) project becomes operational.  A Primary Means Test(MT) on file less than or equal to 1 year old as of the VFA Start Date shall not expire. Otherwise, MTs older than 1 year shall be considered expired and a new MT shall be required.

If populated, this field will be used as the expiration date for the  Special Treatment Authority (STA) for Agent Orange (AO) Veterans with an  AO exposure location of Vietnam.  New enrollments of AO Veterans with  an exposure location of Vietnam and no additional eligibility factors  will no longer be enrolled as a Priority Group 6 if the enrollment date  is greater than the expiration date.

If populated, this field will be used as the expiration date for the  Special Treatment Authority (STA) for Southwest Asia Condition (SWAC)  Veterans.  New enrollments of SWAC Veterans and no additional eligibility  factors will no longer be enrolled as a Priority Group 6 if the  enrollment date is greater than the expiration date.
The PROCESS MVI DOD UPDATE? value indicates whether the site should  process Date of Death (DOD) messages from the Master Veteran Index (MVI).
The date on which "BLUE WATER NAVY" is a valid Agent Orange Exposure Location and can be entered by the user.
